But before we dive into the cool waters of Cloudy and Ethel Creek, we have to talk about a taboo but entirely necessary topic: doing a number two in the bush. Join Blair Woodcock, Cherry Judge, and Luen Warneke from the Townsville Bushwalking Club for another packed episode of the Take A Hike podcast! This week, the crew dives into the great gear debate: are pack liners actually necessary, or can you get away with individual dry bags and a rain cover? Cherry and Luen share their contrasting approaches to keeping gear dry on the trails, the truth about backpack rain covers, and why you might want to avoid dry bags with see-through patches.

Then, the team welcomes avid adventure racer Mitch Nissan to the show. Newly relocated to Townsville from Cairns, Mitch shares his incredible journey of surviving GodZone in New Zealand's South Fiordland - widely regarded as the world's toughest expedition race. Discover what it takes to navigate 550 kilometers of wild terrain, crush 12,000 meters of elevation change over six days, and manage severe sleep deprivation with a 50% team dropout rate.

Mitch opens up about his childhood adventures in Far North Queensland, how bow hunting in Cape York taught him to navigate off-trail, and how his team used their strong paddling background to pass nearly 100 teams in a single leg. Tune in for an inspiring look at extreme endurance and local North Queensland hiking expertise!

Welcome back to the Take A Hike podcast! In this episode, host Blair Woodcock sits down with Cherry Judge and Luen Warneke from the Townsville Bushwalking Club to uncover some of North Queensland's best-kept secrets. If you are looking to escape the hustle and bustle, we are diving deep into the Misty Mountains of Wooroonooran National Park to map out an unforgettable off-track adventure.

Plus, the team wraps up with some local hiking inspiration closer to Townsville, including a weekend trek up Cape Cleveland's Western Ridge to Iron Head. Whether you are planning your next major long weekend getaway or a quick local hike, grab your gear and get ready to explore the wild side of North Queensland.

Join host Blair Woodcock and Townsville Bushwalking Club regulars Luen Warneke and Cherry Judge as they navigate the complexities of sharing our favorite trails online. In this episode, we dive into the heated debate surrounding geotagging in the age of social media. Is it responsible to tag remote locations, or does it invite unintended environmental damage and safety risks? Our experts weigh the benefits of raising awareness against the realities of overcrowding, litter, and the need for trail preservation.

After tackling the debate, we shift our focus to the stunning landscapes of Porcupine Gorge. Perfect for an upcoming long weekend, this spectacular gorge offers a rugged and rewarding experience for those ready to explore. Blair Woodcock, Luen Warneke, and Cherry Judge are back to discuss the wild side of North Queensland hiking! We kick things off with a crazy story about a recent python encounter in Behana Gorge. Are non-venomous snakes something to worry about? The team shares essential snake etiquette for the trails, from heavy footfalls and thick gaiters to using walking sticks to stay safe in the Australian bush.

Next, we head north of Townsville for a deep dive into the Dalrymple Gap walking track. This 10km historical bushwalking route just south of Cardwell offers beautiful rainforest views, stunning creek crossings, and an incredible bridge built with bricks shipped all the way from Scotland. The crew debates the best way to tackle the route - starting from the Cardwell side to get the steep hill out of the way, or braving the Broadwater side for the beautiful creeks.

Plus, we give you a heads-up on native trail hazards like the annoying wait-a-while vine and the dreaded gimpy-gimpy stinging tree, and chat about upcoming weekend adventures to the Russell River area and Diorite Falls. Grab your gaiters, lace up your boots, and get ready for another fantastic bushwalking episode! Read more about this route on our blog at [https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/snake-101-dalrymple-gap/](https://townsvillebushwalkingclub.com/snake-101-dalrymple-gap/)
